steps.title: Calculate the rate of water addition per second
steps.content:
One 0.06-milliliter drop falls every 6 seconds.
Rate per second = $$\frac{0.06 \text{ mL}}{6 \text{ s}} = 0.01 \text{ mL/s}$$

steps.title: Build the volume equation
steps.content:
The container starts with 21 milliliters and gains 0.01 milliliters each second.
After t seconds, the total volume is:
$$v = 21 + 0.01t$$
Rearranging: $$v = 0.01t + 21$$
This equation matches choice C — C is correct.
